data.body:
## Description

**What**: Add a scrutiny:pending tag check at the entry of /preplanning and /implementation-plan — when present, the skill halts and directs the practitioner to run /dso:brainstorm first
**Why**: Unreviewed epics should not silently enter story decomposition or task planning
**Scope**:
- IN: Entry check in both skills for scrutiny:pending tag; halt behavior with redirect message; no planning output produced when tag is present
- OUT: Removing the tag after brainstorm runs (brainstorm handles this); changing the tag contract definition (Story 4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, /preplanning and /implementation-plan check for the scrutiny:pending tag on entry and halt with a message directing the practitioner to run /dso:brainstorm first when the tag is present
  ← Satisfies: "when present, the skill halts directing the practitioner to run /dso:brainstorm first; no planning output is produced"
- When this story is complete, the gate works correctly when invoked via /dso:sprint (not just standalone invocation)
  ← Satisfies: "no planning output is produced"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD DoD

## Considerations

- [Consumer Impact] /dso:sprint also invokes /preplanning and /implementation-plan — the gate must fire in that context too. /dso:brainstorm also invokes these skills but brainstorm-created epics will never have scrutiny:pending (brainstorm runs full scrutiny), so no false-positive path exists
- [Reliability] Use presence-based check (only block when scrutiny:pending is explicitly present), not absence-based — existing epics must not be retroactively blocked
